query: My rice field has hopperburn with yellowing and drying at the base of the tillers, and I've been applying extra nitrogen. What pest is this?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Brown Planthopper in Rice. Brown planthopper (Nilaparvata lugens) is a sap-sucking insect that congregates at the base of rice tillers, causing a rapid yellowing and drying symptom known as hopperburn. Populations build quickly under high humidity and excess nitrogen application. Recommended control includes avoiding excess nitrogen, maintaining alternate wetting and drying irrigation, conserving natural enemies such as mirid bugs and spiders, and applying imidacloprid or a recommended neonicotinoid only when populations exceed the economic threshold of 5-10 hoppers per hill.

query: There are spindle shaped grey lesions with brown margins on my rice leaves, and now even the panicle neck looks infected. What disease is it?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Rice Blast Disease. Rice blast, caused by the fungus Magnaporthe oryzae, produces spindle-shaped lesions with grey centers and brown margins on leaves, and can also infect the neck of the panicle causing neck blast, which cuts grain filling severely. High nitrogen doses and dense planting favor the disease. Management includes using resistant varieties, balanced nitrogen application, seed treatment with tricyclazole, and foliar sprays of tricyclazole or isoprothiolane at early symptom onset.

query: My rice leaves have water soaked yellow lesions running along the margins after heavy rain, what is this disease?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Bacterial Leaf Blight in Rice. Bacterial leaf blight, caused by Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae, enters through leaf wounds and hydathodes, producing water-soaked lesions that turn yellow to white and run along leaf margins. In severe seedling infections it causes a wilting phase called kresek. The disease spreads rapidly in wind-driven rain and standing floodwater. Management relies on resistant varieties, balanced nitrogen, avoiding leaf injury during transplanting, and copper oxychloride sprays combined with streptocycline where locally approved.

query: My wheat leaves have yellow-orange powdery pustules arranged in stripes, what is this?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Yellow (Stripe) Rust of Wheat. Yellow rust, also called stripe rust, is caused by Puccinia striiformis and appears as yellow-orange powdery pustules arranged in stripes along leaf veins, favored by cool, humid weather. Severe infection reduces photosynthetic area and grain filling. Management includes growing rust-resistant varieties, timely sowing to avoid peak disease-favorable temperature windows, and foliar fungicide sprays of propiconazole or tebuconazole once pustules are first observed on the lower canopy.

query: There are small green insects clustering on my wheat ear heads and leaves excreting a sticky substance, what pest is this?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Aphid Infestation in Wheat. Wheat aphids (including Sitobion avenae and Rhopalosiphum species) cluster on leaves and ear heads, sucking sap and excreting honeydew that promotes sooty mould growth, and can also transmit barley yellow dwarf virus. Cool, dry conditions in the vegetative to earhead stage favor buildup. Conserving natural predators such as ladybird beetles and syrphid flies is the first line of defense; imidacloprid or dimethoate sprays are recommended only when aphid counts exceed the local economic threshold.

query: Patches of my crop are wilting and dying even though there has been no drought, and I found white ant-like insects at the roots.
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Termite Damage in Field Crops. Termites (commonly Odontotermes obesus in South Asian fields) attack the root system and lower stem of standing crops, especially under moisture-stressed and sandy soils, causing wilting and plant death in patches that can be mistaken for drought stress. Well-decomposed farmyard manure free of termite colonies, avoiding un-decomposed organic matter incorporation, and seed or soil treatment with chlorpyrifos at sowing are the standard preventive measures.

query: Tiny white insects fly up from the underside of my cotton leaves when I disturb the plant, and there's a black sooty coating on the leaves.
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Whitefly in Cotton. Whitefly (Bemisia tabaci) adults and nymphs feed on the underside of cotton leaves, excreting honeydew that supports sooty mould, and are also the vector for cotton leaf curl virus. Populations peak in warm, dry weather. Management includes yellow sticky traps for monitoring, conserving natural enemies, avoiding excess nitrogen, and applying diafenthiuron or a recommended systemic insecticide in rotation to reduce resistance buildup, respecting the pre-harvest interval.

query: My cotton leaves are curling downward at the margins and turning yellow-bronze, what is causing this?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Cotton Leaf Curl Virus Disease. Cotton leaf curl virus disease is caused by a whitefly-transmitted begomovirus complex, producing upward or downward leaf curling, thickened veins, and leaf-like enations on the underside of leaves, which severely stunts plant growth and reduces boll formation. There is no direct cure once infected; management is centered on controlling the whitefly vector, removing and destroying infected plants early, and planting tolerant cotton varieties recommended for the region.

query: My tomato leaves have water soaked patches turning brown with a pale halo and white growth underneath in this humid weather, what is happening?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Late Blight in Tomato. Late blight, caused by the oomycete Phytophthora infestans, produces irregular water-soaked lesions on leaves that quickly turn brown with a pale green halo, and a white fungal growth on the underside in humid conditions; fruit develop firm brown blotches. Cool, wet weather drives rapid spread and can destroy a field within days. Management includes wide plant spacing for airflow, avoiding overhead irrigation in the evening, and preventive sprays of mancozeb or a systemic fungicide like metalaxyl once disease-favorable weather is forecast.

query: There are round holes in my tomato fruit with some frass near the entry point, what pest is this?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Fruit Borer in Tomato. The fruit borer (Helicoverpa armigera), also called the American bollworm on cotton, bores a round entry hole into tomato fruit and feeds internally, leaving frass near the entry point and making fruit unmarketable. Pheromone traps at 5 per acre help monitor moth activity. Management includes hand-picking and destroying infested fruit, releasing Trichogramma egg parasitoids, and applying a recommended insecticide such as chlorantraniliprole or emamectin benzoate at first sign of fresh damage.

query: My potato leaves are rotting with dark lesions and a white ring on the underside, and I'm worried about the tubers, what should I do?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Late Blight in Potato. Potato late blight, caused by Phytophthora infestans, causes dark water-soaked lesions on leaves with a white fungal ring on the underside in humid weather, and can rot tubers in storage if spores wash down to the soil. Cool, humid, overcast weather triggers rapid epidemics that can destroy a crop within a week. Management includes earthing up to protect tubers from spores, avoiding overhead irrigation, and preventive fungicide sprays of mancozeb followed by a systemic fungicide such as cymoxanil once the disease is confirmed.

query: What is the general strategy for reducing pesticide use while still controlling pests effectively?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Integrated Pest Management Principles. Integrated Pest Management (IPM) combines regular field scouting, economic threshold-based decision-making, cultural practices such as crop rotation and resistant varieties, biological control through natural enemies, and chemical pesticides used only as a last resort at the correct dose and timing. The goal is to keep pest populations below the level causing economic damage while minimizing pesticide use, resistance development, and harm to beneficial insects and the environment. Farmer field schools are a common extension method for teaching IPM decision-making.

query: My rice plants are stunted with yellow-orange leaves and low tillering, and I've seen green leafhoppers around, what disease could this be?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Rice Tungro Disease. Rice tungro disease is caused by a combination of two viruses, Rice Tungro Bacilliform Virus and Rice Tungro Spherical Virus, transmitted by the green leafhopper (Nephotettix virescens). Infected plants show yellow-orange leaf discoloration, stunting, and reduced tillering, with yield loss depending on infection stage. There is no curative treatment; management is preventive, focusing on synchronized planting, removing infected volunteer plants and grassy weeds that harbor the vector, and using tungro-tolerant varieties.

query: Some of my wheat grains have a black powdery mass with a foul fishy smell but the grain isn't fully destroyed, what is this?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Karnal Bunt of Wheat. Karnal bunt, caused by the fungus Tilletia indica, is a partial bunt of wheat grains where infected kernels develop a fish-odor black powdery mass replacing part of the endosperm, without necessarily destroying the whole grain. It is primarily a quality and quarantine concern rather than a major yield-loss disease, since infection is usually partial. Management includes seed treatment with carboxin or tebuconazole, crop rotation with non-cereal crops, and avoiding irrigation during the flowering to early grain-fill window when spores infect florets.

query: I opened a cotton boll and found the petals webbed together with larval silk in a rosette shape, and pin-sized holes in the boll.
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Pink Bollworm in Cotton. Pink bollworm (Pectinophora gossypiella) larvae bore into cotton flower buds and bolls, feeding on developing seeds and lint, and characteristically cause a 'rosette flower' symptom where petals are webbed together by larval silk. Infested bolls show pin-sized entry holes and internal staining. Management includes destroying crop residue and unopened bolls after harvest, pheromone traps for monitoring, avoiding extra-early or extra-late sowing, and using recommended Bt cotton hybrids alongside a non-Bt refuge to delay resistance.

query: My cotton leaves are curling upward with thickened veins and small leaf-like growths on the underside, what is this?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Cotton Leaf Curl Virus Disease. Cotton leaf curl virus disease is caused by a whitefly-transmitted begomovirus complex, producing upward or downward leaf curling, thickened veins, and leaf-like enations on the underside of leaves, which severely stunts plant growth and reduces boll formation. There is no direct cure once infected; management is centered on controlling the whitefly vector, removing and destroying infected plants early, and planting tolerant cotton varieties recommended for the region.

query: My young tomato seedlings have severely curled, crinkled small leaves and are badly stunted, what is causing this?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Tomato Leaf Curl Virus. Tomato leaf curl virus, a whitefly-transmitted begomovirus, causes upward curling and crinkling of leaflets, reduced leaf size, and severe stunting when plants are infected young, often resulting in near-total fruit loss in early infections. There is no chemical cure for infected plants. Management focuses on raising seedlings under insect-proof nylon mesh nurseries, roguing out infected plants early, and controlling the whitefly vector population around the nursery and main field.

query: My ripening chili fruit have sunken circular water soaked spots with dark rings inside them, what disease is this?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Anthracnose Fruit Rot in Chili. Anthracnose fruit rot, caused by Colletotrichum species, produces sunken, circular, water-soaked spots on ripening chili fruit that enlarge and develop concentric rings of dark fungal fruiting bodies, causing major post-harvest loss in humid regions. Infection is favored by warm, wet weather during fruit ripening. Management includes using disease-free seed, removing and destroying infected fruit, and preventive sprays of carbendazim or a copper-based fungicide starting at fruit set.

query: When I split open my sugarcane stalk, the inside is reddish with white cross bands and smells alcoholic, what is wrong?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Red Rot of Sugarcane. Red rot, caused by Colletotrichum falcatum, is one of the most destructive sugarcane diseases, producing internal reddening of the stalk interspersed with white cross bands visible when the cane is split open, along with an alcoholic smell from fermenting juice. Infected setts are the primary source of spread. Management relies heavily on planting disease-free setts of resistant varieties, hot-water treatment of seed setts, and destroying infected clumps and crop residue rather than chemical control, since fungicides give limited field-level protection.

query: My young sugarcane shoots are dying from the center with a foul smell and come out easily when pulled, what pest is this?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Early Shoot Borer in Sugarcane. Early shoot borer (Chilo infuscatellus) larvae bore into young sugarcane shoots at ground level, producing a characteristic deadheart with a foul smell when the central shoot is pulled out and gives way easily. Infestation is highest in the first three months after planting. Management includes removing and destroying deadhearts, releasing Trichogramma chilonis parasitoids, and applying a recommended granular insecticide such as carbofuran or chlorantraniliprole at planting and again at the peak infestation window.

query: My stored potato tubers have tunnels with frass inside them, what pest caused this?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Potato Tuber Moth. Potato tuber moth (Phthorimaea operculella) larvae mine leaves and tunnel into exposed tubers both in the field and in storage, leaving frass-filled galleries that make tubers unfit for sale or seed use. Infestation risk rises when tubers are exposed by cracked ridges or in poorly covered storage heaps. Management includes deep and thorough earthing up, avoiding tuber exposure, and storing seed tubers with dried neem leaves or approved storage-grade insecticide dust.

query: There's window-pane style feeding damage and sawdust-like waste inside the whorl of my young maize plants, what pest is this?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Fall Armyworm in Maize. Fall armyworm (Spodoptera frugiperda) is an invasive pest whose larvae feed inside the whorl of young maize plants, leaving characteristic window-pane feeding damage and coarse sawdust-like frass in the whorl, and can cause severe yield loss if uncontrolled at early vegetative stages. It has a wide host range and disperses over long distances as an adult moth. Management includes early scouting of the whorl, hand-crushing egg masses, and applying emamectin benzoate or spinetoram directed into the whorl once damage crosses the threshold.

query: Which systemic fungicide is commonly used for seed treatment and against anthracnose and leaf spot diseases?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Carbendazim Use Guidance. Carbendazim is a systemic benzimidazole fungicide absorbed by plant tissue, effective against a range of fungal diseases including anthracnose, leaf spots, and wilt-causing pathogens, and is also used as a seed treatment to protect germinating seedlings. Because resistance develops relatively easily in fungal populations exposed repeatedly to benzimidazole fungicides, it should be used in rotation or combination with a fungicide from a different mode-of-action group, following the labeled dose and harvest interval.

query: I have a highly persistent beetle infestation in my stored wheat that survives for a long time even without food, what is this pest?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Khapra Beetle in Stored Grain. Khapra beetle (Trogoderma granarium) is a highly destructive storage pest of wheat, rice, and other stored grains, surviving long periods without food and hiding in cracks, making it extremely difficult to eradicate once established in a storage structure. It is a quarantine pest of major international trade concern. Management includes thorough cleaning and sealing of storage structures before filling, fumigation with an approved fumigant such as aluminium phosphide under expert supervision, and regular inspection of stored grain for larval skins and damage.

query: Mere kapas ke patton par tela lag gaya hai aur pattiyan neeche ki taraf mud rahi hain.
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Brown Planthopper in Rice. Brown planthopper (Nilaparvata lugens) is a sap-sucking insect that congregates at the base of rice tillers, causing a rapid yellowing and drying symptom known as hopperburn. Populations build quickly under high humidity and excess nitrogen application. Recommended control includes avoiding excess nitrogen, maintaining alternate wetting and drying irrigation, conserving natural enemies such as mirid bugs and spiders, and applying imidacloprid or a recommended neonicotinoid only when populations exceed the economic threshold of 5-10 hoppers per hill.

query: Bhindi ke patton par hara tela dikh raha hai, kya spray karu?
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Jassid (Leafhopper) in Cotton and Okra. Jassids (Amrasca biguttula biguttula) are small wedge-shaped leafhoppers that feed on the underside of cotton and okra leaves, injecting toxic saliva that causes leaf margins to curl downward and turn yellow-bronze, a symptom called hopperburn. Hairy-leaved varieties suffer less damage. Management includes growing recommended tolerant varieties, conserving spiders and predatory bugs, and spraying imidacloprid or acetamiprid when nymphs exceed 2 per leaf on three consecutive counts.

query: Kapas ke patton ke niche safed makkhi bahut zyada hai aur kaali fungus bhi lag gayi hai.

Populations peak in warm, dry weather. Management includes yellow sticky traps for monitoring, conserving natural enemies, avoiding excess nitrogen, and applying diafenthiuron or a recommended systemic insecticide in rotation to reduce resistance buildup, respecting the pre-harvest interval.

query: Dhaan ki fasal me tana chhedak lag gaya hai, beech ka ankur sookh gaya hai.
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Yellow Stem Borer in Rice. Yellow stem borer (Scirpophaga incertulas) larvae bore into rice stems, producing a 'deadheart' symptom (central shoot drying) during the vegetative stage and a 'whitehead' symptom (empty, sterile panicle) during the reproductive stage. Pheromone traps help monitor moth activity. Clipping and destroying egg masses, releasing Trichogramma egg parasitoids, and applying cartap hydrochloride or chlorantraniliprole at the recommended dose when deadhearts exceed 5 percent are standard integrated management steps.

query: Bhindi ke patton par safed dhool rog lag gaya hai.
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Powdery Mildew in Vegetable Crops. Powdery mildew appears as a white, powdery fungal growth on the upper and lower leaf surface of vegetable crops such as cucurbits, okra, and chili, eventually causing leaf yellowing, curling, and premature drying. It spreads fastest in warm days with cool nights and high humidity, without needing free water on the leaf. Management includes wettable sulphur sprays, avoiding excess nitrogen, ensuring adequate plant spacing for airflow, and hexaconazole or myclobutanil sprays for established infections.

query: Dhaan ke paudhe baune reh gaye hain aur patte peele-santre range ke ho gaye hain, hariyal tiddey bhi dikhe.
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Yellow Stem Borer in Rice. Yellow stem borer (Scirpophaga incertulas) larvae bore into rice stems, producing a 'deadheart' symptom (central shoot drying) during the vegetative stage and a 'whitehead' symptom (empty, sterile panicle) during the reproductive stage. Pheromone traps help monitor moth activity. Clipping and destroying egg masses, releasing Trichogramma egg parasitoids, and applying cartap hydrochloride or chlorantraniliprole at the recommended dose when deadhearts exceed 5 percent are standard integrated management steps.

query: Makke ke paudhon ki gobhi mein tambaku sundi ka prakop hai, chura jaisa mal dikh raha hai.
answer: Based on your query, this most likely matches: Fall Armyworm in Maize. Fall armyworm (Spodoptera frugiperda) is an invasive pest whose larvae feed inside the whorl of young maize plants, leaving characteristic window-pane feeding damage and coarse sawdust-like frass in the whorl, and can cause severe yield loss if uncontrolled at early vegetative stages. It has a wide host range and disperses over long distances as an adult moth. Management includes early scouting of the whorl, hand-crushing egg masses, and applying emamectin benzoate or spinetoram directed into the whorl once damage crosses the threshold.
